manPages.date:
DATE(1)                      GregOS Manual                      DATE(1)

NAME
       date - display the current date and time

SYNOPSIS
       date [-u]

DESCRIPTION
       date displays the current date and time. With -u, displays
       the time in UTC instead of the local timezone.

OPTIONS
       -u     Display time in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC).

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       uptime(1)
manPages.echo:
ECHO(1)                      GregOS Manual                      ECHO(1)

NAME
       echo - display a line of text

SYNOPSIS
       echo [string ...]

DESCRIPTION
       echo writes its arguments to standard output, separated by
       spaces, followed by a newline.

       Shell variables $USER, $HOME, $SHELL, and $HOSTNAME are
       expanded.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       env(1)
manPages.uname:
UNAME(1)                     GregOS Manual                     UNAME(1)

NAME
       uname - print system information

SYNOPSIS
       uname [-a | -s | -n | -r | -v | -m]

DESCRIPTION
       uname prints information about the system.

OPTIONS
       -s     Kernel name (default)
       -n     Network hostname
       -r     Kernel release
       -v     Kernel version
       -m     Machine hardware name
       -a     All of the above

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       hostname(1)
manPages.wc:
WC(1)                        GregOS Manual                        WC(1)

NAME
       wc - word, line, and byte count

SYNOPSIS
       wc [-l | -w | -c] <file>

DESCRIPTION
       wc counts lines, words, and characters in the specified
       file. Without flags, all three counts are shown.

OPTIONS
       -l     Count lines only
       -w     Count words only
       -c     Count characters only

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       cat(1), head(1)
manPages.head:
HEAD(1)                      GregOS Manual                      HEAD(1)

NAME
       head - output the first part of files

SYNOPSIS
       head [-n count] <file>

DESCRIPTION
       head displays the first count lines of the specified file.
       If -n is not given, the default is 10 lines.

OPTIONS
       -n count   Number of lines to display.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       cat(1), wc(1)
manPages.grep:
GREP(1)                      GregOS Manual                      GREP(1)

NAME
       grep - search file contents for a pattern

SYNOPSIS
       grep [-n] <pattern> [file]

DESCRIPTION
       grep searches for lines matching pattern in the specified
       file. If no file is given, all root-level files are
       searched. Patterns are case-insensitive regular
       expressions.

OPTIONS
       -n     Prefix each matching line with its line number.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       cat(1), wc(1)
manPages.uptime:
UPTIME(1)                    GregOS Manual                    UPTIME(1)

NAME
       uptime - show how long the system has been running

SYNOPSIS
       uptime

DESCRIPTION
       uptime displays the current time, how long the system has
       been running since boot, the number of users, and system
       load averages.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       date(1), ps(1)
manPages.hostname:
HOSTNAME(1)                  GregOS Manual                  HOSTNAME(1)

NAME
       hostname - show the system hostname

SYNOPSIS
       hostname

DESCRIPTION
       hostname displays the name of the current host system.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       uname(1)
manPages.id:
ID(1)                        GregOS Manual                        ID(1)

NAME
       id - display user identity

SYNOPSIS
       id

DESCRIPTION
       id prints the user and group IDs of the current user.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       whoami(1)
manPages.ps:
PS(1)                        GregOS Manual                        PS(1)

NAME
       ps - report process status

SYNOPSIS
       ps [aux]

DESCRIPTION
       ps displays information about active processes. Without
       arguments, only processes attached to the current terminal
       are shown. With aux, all system processes are listed.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       uptime(1)
manPages.df:
DF(1)                        GregOS Manual                        DF(1)

NAME
       df - report filesystem disk space usage

SYNOPSIS
       df [-h]

DESCRIPTION
       df displays the amount of disk space used and available
       on mounted filesystems.

OPTIONS
       -h     Print sizes in human-readable format (e.g. 1G).

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       free(1)
manPages.free:
FREE(1)                      GregOS Manual                      FREE(1)

NAME
       free - display amount of free and used memory

SYNOPSIS
       free [-h]

DESCRIPTION
       free displays the total amount of free and used physical
       memory in the system.

OPTIONS
       -h     Print sizes in human-readable format.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       df(1), ps(1)
manPages.env:
ENV(1)                       GregOS Manual                       ENV(1)

NAME
       env - display environment variables

SYNOPSIS
       env

DESCRIPTION
       env prints the current environment variables for the
       active shell session.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       echo(1)
manPages.ls:
LS(1)                        GregOS Manual                        LS(1)

NAME
       ls - list directory contents

SYNOPSIS
       ls [-a]

DESCRIPTION
       ls lists the contents of the current directory. At the
       root level, text files and subdirectories are shown.

OPTIONS
       -a     Include hidden files (dotfiles).

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       cd(1), cat(1)
manPages.cd:
CD(1)                        GregOS Manual                        CD(1)

NAME
       cd - change working directory

SYNOPSIS
       cd [directory]

DESCRIPTION
       cd changes the current working directory. Without
       arguments, returns to the home directory. Use .. to
       go up one level.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       ls(1), pwd(1)
manPages.pwd:
PWD(1)                       GregOS Manual                       PWD(1)

NAME
       pwd - print working directory

SYNOPSIS
       pwd

DESCRIPTION
       pwd prints the full pathname of the current working
       directory.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       cd(1), ls(1)
manPages.cat:
CAT(1)                       GregOS Manual                       CAT(1)

NAME
       cat - concatenate and display files

SYNOPSIS
       cat <file>

DESCRIPTION
       cat reads the specified file and prints its contents to
       standard output.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       head(1), grep(1)
manPages.open:
OPEN(1)                      GregOS Manual                      OPEN(1)

NAME
       open - open a file or directory in the browser

SYNOPSIS
       open [file]

DESCRIPTION
       open launches the specified file in the browser. Without
       arguments, opens the current directory. Navigate into a
       project folder with cd, then run open to launch it.

EXAMPLES
       cd games && open beary-time.html
       cd games && open

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       cd(1), ls(1)
manPages.whoami:
WHOAMI(1)                    GregOS Manual                    WHOAMI(1)

NAME
       whoami - display system and user information

SYNOPSIS
       whoami

DESCRIPTION
       whoami displays information about the current user
       session including operating system, browser, language,
       screen resolution, and local time.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       id(1), hostname(1)
manPages.history:
HISTORY(1)                   GregOS Manual                   HISTORY(1)

NAME
       history - display command history

SYNOPSIS
       history

DESCRIPTION
       history displays the list of commands entered during the
       current session, numbered from most recent.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       help(1)
manPages.clear:
CLEAR(1)                     GregOS Manual                     CLEAR(1)

NAME
       clear - clear the terminal screen

SYNOPSIS
       clear

DESCRIPTION
       clear removes all previous output from the terminal
       display.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       help(1)
manPages.su:
SU(1)                        GregOS Manual                        SU(1)

NAME
       su - switch user

SYNOPSIS
       su <username>

DESCRIPTION
       su switches the current session to the specified user
       account. The prompt, home directory, and environment
       variables are updated to reflect the new identity.

       Use 'exit' to return to the previous user.

AUTHENTICATION
       Access to user accounts requires appropriate system
       privileges. Inactive accounts may require additional
       authorization.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       finger(1), id(1), exit(1)
manPages.mail:
MAIL(1)                      GregOS Manual                      MAIL(1)

NAME
       mail - read mail

SYNOPSIS
       mail

DESCRIPTION
       mail reads the current user's mailbox from /var/mail.
       Messages are displayed in received order.

AUTHOR
       GregOS System

SEE ALSO
       su(1), finger(1)
manPages.pkg:
PKG(1)                       GregOS Manual                       PKG(1)

NAME
       pkg - GregOS package manager

SYNOPSIS
       pkg update
       pkg list
       pkg install <package>
       pkg installed

DESCRIPTION
       pkg manages the installation of utility packages from
       the GregOS repository. Packages are installed to ~/bin
       and become available as commands.

COMMANDS
       update      Synchronize the package repository index.
                   Must be run before list or install.

       list        Display available packages with version,
                   size, and installation status.

       install     Download and install a package by name.

       installed   Show currently installed packages.

AUTHOR
       GregOS Package System

SEE ALSO
       man(1)
manPages.mount:
MOUNT(8)                     GregOS Manual                     MOUNT(8)

NAME
       mount - mount a filesystem

SYNOPSIS
       mount [device]

DESCRIPTION
       Without arguments, mount displays currently mounted
       filesystems. With a device argument, attempts to mount
       the specified device.

       Devices from the v1.0 ROM migration are listed in
       migration.conf. Pending devices may require manual
       mounting.

DIAGNOSTICS
       If a device fault occurs, check dmesg(1) for details.

NOTES
       CONFIG_STRICT_DEVTYPE=n in this build. Mount does not
       validate device type before read. Block, character, and
       unregistered devices are all accepted.

       Standard behavior rejects non-block devices before the
       read syscall. See kernel config.

BUGS
       Mounting non-block devices may issue invalid I/O to the
       device driver. Data corruption or hardware damage may
       result.

FILES
       /etc/migration.conf    Device migration manifest

AUTHOR
       GregOS Kernel Team

SEE ALSO
       dmesg(1), df(1), rf0(4)
manPages.decode:
DECODE(1)                    GregOS Manual                    DECODE(1)

NAME
       decode - convert hex or base64 to ASCII

SYNOPSIS
       decode --hex <string>
       decode --b64 <string>

DESCRIPTION
       decode takes a string of hex digits or a base64-encoded
       string and prints the ASCII representation to standard
       output. Each pair of hex digits is treated as one byte.
       Input that is not valid hex or base64 will produce an
       error.

OPTIONS
       --hex   Read input as hexadecimal. Each pair of characters
               (0-9, a-f) is converted to one ASCII character.
               For example, 41 = "A", 7a = "z".

       --b64   Read input as base64.

EXAMPLES
       $ decode --hex 48656c6c6f
       Hello

       $ decode --hex 4f4b
       OK

AUTHOR
       Gregory Alan, CS 240 Fall 1980

BUGS
       None known.
manPages.rot13:
ROT13(1)                     GregOS Manual                     ROT13(1)

NAME
       rot13 - rotate ASCII letters by 13 positions

SYNOPSIS
       rot13 <text>

DESCRIPTION
       rot13 applies the ROT13 substitution cipher to the input
       text. Each letter is replaced by the letter 13 positions
       after it in the alphabet, wrapping around from z to a.
       Non-alphabetic characters are passed through unchanged.

       ROT13 is its own inverse: applying it twice returns the
       original text. It is not encryption. It is not even close
       to encryption. Do not use this for anything important.

EXAMPLES
       $ rot13 Hello
       Uryyb

       $ rot13 Uryyb
       Hello

AUTHOR
       Gregory Alan, CS 101 Spring 1979

BUGS
       None known.
manPages.freq:
FREQ(1)                      GregOS Manual                      FREQ(1)

NAME
       freq - display letter frequency histogram

SYNOPSIS
       freq <text>

DESCRIPTION
       freq counts the frequency of each alphabetic character in
       the input and displays a histogram sorted by frequency in
       descending order. Character counts and percentages are
       shown alongside each bar.

       Non-alphabetic characters are ignored. All comparisons
       are case-insensitive.

EXAMPLES
       $ freq the quick brown fox
       (displays histogram)

AUTHOR
       Gregory Alan, MATH 385 Fall 1981

SEE ALSO
       entropy(1), rot13(1)
manPages.entropy:
ENTROPY(1)                   GregOS Manual                   ENTROPY(1)

NAME
       entropy - compute Shannon entropy of input

SYNOPSIS
       entropy <text>

DESCRIPTION
       entropy computes the Shannon entropy of the input string
       in bits per symbol. Higher entropy indicates more
       randomness or information content.

       Also reports: input length, number of unique symbols,
       and the theoretical maximum entropy for the observed
       alphabet size.

EXAMPLES
       $ entropy aaaa
       Shannon entropy: 0.0000 bits/symbol

       $ entropy abcd
       Shannon entropy: 2.0000 bits/symbol

AUTHOR
       Gregory Alan, CS 432 Spring 1982

SEE ALSO
       freq(1)
manPages.crc:
CRC(1)                       GregOS Manual                       CRC(1)

NAME
       crc - compute CRC32 checksum

SYNOPSIS
       crc <text>

DESCRIPTION
       crc computes the CRC32 checksum of the input string and
       prints the result as an 8-digit hexadecimal value.

       Uses the standard CRC32 polynomial (0xEDB88320, reflected).

EXAMPLES
       $ crc hello
       3610a686

AUTHOR
       Gregory Alan, CS 372 Fall 1981

SEE ALSO
       decode(1)
manPages.strings:
STRINGS(1)                   GregOS Manual                   STRINGS(1)

NAME
       strings - extract printable strings from files

SYNOPSIS
       strings <file>

DESCRIPTION
       strings scans the specified file and prints all sequences
       of printable characters that are at least 4 characters
       long. Useful for examining binary files.

EXAMPLES
       $ strings /usr/bin/ls
       (printable strings from binary)

AUTHOR
       Gregory Alan, Summer internship 1982

SEE ALSO
       decode(1)
